summary: Solarix backend smoke test (26 endpoints exercised across 2 freshly-registered companies). 26/27 checks pass. Multi-tenant isolation, JWT (cookie + Bearer), client SOL-2026 IDs, 7-stage progress + auto Handover Complete, Emergent Object Storage upload/download with tenant isolation, employees, notifications, activity logs, and company profile all working. One real bug: employee login via employee_id fails because login lowercases the identifier but employee_id is stored uppercase.
passed: POST /api/auth/register (Company A and Company B, 15-day trial, JWT in body+cookie)
GET /api/company, PUT /api/company (Admin) with website update verified
PATCH /api/clients/{id}/stages -> progress=100 and status auto-set to 'Handover Complete'
POST /api/files/upload (PNG via Emergent Object Storage) and GET /api/files/{id}
Multi-tenant isolation: B sees 0 of A's clients; cross-company GET client/file returns 404

failed.issue: POST /api/auth/login with identifier=employee_id always returns 401, even when password is correct. Repro: register company, create employee (server generates employee_id like 'EMP-2026-DB4B25'), then POST /api/auth/login with identifier='EMP-2026-DB4B25' OR 'emp-2026-db4b25' -> both 401 Invalid credentials.
failed.evidence: Root cause: server.py login() does `ident = data.identifier.lower().strip()` then queries `{"employee_id": ident}`. employee_id values are generated/stored uppercase (uuid4().hex[:6].upper()), so a lowercased identifier never matches the uppercase stored employee_id. Email/mobile login work fine because they are stored lowercase/digits.

action_items: Fix employee_id login in /app/backend/server.py POST /api/auth/login: either (a) don't lowercase when matching employee_id (e.g., query with case-insensitive regex or add an upper() branch in the $or), or (b) store employee_id in lowercase, or (c) use a case-insensitive collation for the employee_id field.
